title: Make TOP part of Sym
body:
This is a hacked up version of #3032. Please don't look too much at the changes to Verilator, it's a hack and still messy, but in the final version, Verilator internals should be cleaner than before.

Does not work with --trace or --threads, but single threaded models without tracing should work. In particular, this is good enought to build OpenTitan as such.

Try t/t_EXAMPLE for a basic test to diff the output, or t_cover_line_sc for a slightly more complicated test that also uses SystemC.

There is a seemingly non-trivial peformance improvement (1.5% on Open Titan), which is possibly there because referring back to the TOP from a submodule no longer needs an additional pointer dereference (see change in V3Descope), but might just be my branch predictor tables aliasing differently with this change... The instruction count certainly is lower though:

```
// master (ec1c1127):

      63,793.67 msec task-clock                #    1.000 CPUs utilized            ( +-  0.04% )
             17      context-switches          #    0.000 K/sec                    ( +-  1.86% )
              0      cpu-migrations            #    0.000 K/sec
          1,433      page-faults               #    0.022 K/sec
223,150,544,950      cycles                    #    3.498 GHz                      ( +-  0.04% )
210,712,366,259      instructions              #    0.94  insn per cycle           ( +-  0.00% )
 11,901,574,410      branches                  #  186.564 M/sec                    ( +-  0.00% )
    254,610,603      branch-misses             #    2.14% of all branches          ( +-  0.56% )

        63.7953 +- 0.0260 seconds time elapsed  ( +-  0.04% )

// TOP in Sym (this PR):

      62,821.37 msec task-clock                #    1.000 CPUs utilized            ( +-  0.04% )
             18      context-switches          #    0.000 K/sec                    ( +-  6.00% )
              0      cpu-migrations            #    0.000 K/sec
          1,435      page-faults               #    0.023 K/sec
219,749,164,760      cycles                    #    3.498 GHz                      ( +-  0.04% )
210,050,604,502      instructions              #    0.96  insn per cycle           ( +-  0.00% )
 11,886,067,472      branches                  #  189.204 M/sec                    ( +-  0.01% )
    241,357,352      branch-misses             #    2.03% of all branches          ( +-  0.61% )

        62.8239 +- 0.0232 seconds time elapsed  ( +-  0.04% )
```

Completing this change would require putting one-off internals like tracedump routines and threadpool handles/setup into the Syms (as the top module is no longer special and there naturally is only one instance of Syms for a model), and forwarding every public symbol/entry point from the entry class (wrapper) to the appropriate place.

Shall we proceed to make this proper? As I mentioned in #3032, it would also faciliate further work due to the cleaner internals/less special cases and separtion of concerns in EmitC.
